   234→            # is_file check below classify the missing artifact explicitly.
   235→            artifact = contained(candidate, repo_root, must_exist=False)
   236→        except (ContractError, OSError) as exc:
   237→            findings.append({"node": node_id, "code": "artifact_path_invalid", "detail": str(exc)})
   238→            continue
   239→        if not artifact.is_file():
   240→            findings.append({"node": node_id, "code": "artifact_missing", "detail": raw_path})
   241→            continue
   242→        try:
   243→            frontmatter = frontmatter_of(artifact)
   244→        except ContractError as exc:
   245→            findings.append({"node": node_id, "code": "frontmatter_invalid", "detail": str(exc)})
   246→            continue
   247→        for key in sorted(required_frontmatter - set(frontmatter)):
   248→            findings.append({"node": node_id, "code": "frontmatter_missing", "detail": key})
   249→        for key in ("graph_node_id", "artifact_kind", "file_path", "template_id", "template_version"):
   250→            if key in node and frontmatter.get(key) != node.get(key):
   251→                findings.append({"node": node_id, "code": "frontmatter_parity_error", "detail": key})
   252→    return findings
   253→
   254→
   255→def domain_findings(nodes: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
   256→    findings: list[dict[str, str]] = []
   257→    ids: list[str] = []
   258→    by_id: dict[str, dict[str, Any]] = {}
   259→    for index, node in enumerate(nodes):
   260→        node_id = node.get("graph_node_id") or node.get("id")
   261→        loc = str(node_id or f"nodes[{index}]")
   262→        if not isinstance(node_id, str) or not node_id:
   263→            findings.append({"node": loc, "code": "missing_id", "detail": "graph_node_id required"})
   264→            continue
   265→        ids.append(node_id)
   266→        by_id[node_id] = node
   267→        kind = node.get("artifact_kind", node.get("kind"))
   268→        if kind is not None and kind not in ROOT_BY_KIND:
   269→            findings.append({"node": loc, "code": "invalid_kind", "detail": str(kind)})
   270→        if node.get("status") not in {"draft", "active", "blocked", "done", "closed", "tombstoned"}:
   271→            findings.append({"node": loc, "code": "invalid_status", "detail": str(node.get("status"))})
   272→        dependencies = node.get("depends_on", [])
   273→        if not isinstance(dependencies, list) or any(not isinstance(value, str) for value in dependencies):
   274→            findings.append({"node": loc, "code": "invalid_dependencies", "detail": "depends_on must be string[]"})
   275→        if node.get("tracker_binding") == "beads" and (node.get("github_publication") or {}).get("mode") != "local_only":
   276→            findings.append({"node": loc, "code": "beads_publication", "detail": "beads requires local_only"})
   277→        if node.get("tracker_binding") == "github" and node.get("beads_linkage") is not None:
   278→            findings.append({"node": loc, "code": "binding_collision", "detail": "github cannot have beads_linkage"})
   279→        if node.get("status") == "active":
   280→            readiness = (node.get("implementation_readiness") or {}).get("status")
   281→            if node.get("confirmation_status") != "confirmed" or node.get("evaluation_status") != "pass" or readiness != "complete":
   282→                findings.append({"node": loc, "code": "active_not_ready", "detail": "active requires confirmed/pass/complete"})
   283→        if node.get("tracker_binding") == "repo-config-default":
   284→            findings.append({"node": loc, "code": "unresolved_tracker_binding", "detail": "durable graph cannot retain repo-config-default"})
   285→    for duplicate, count in Counter(ids).items():
   286→        if count > 1:
   287→            findings.append({"node": duplicate, "code": "duplicate_id", "detail": str(count)})
   288→    reference_fields = ("depends_on", "related_nodes", "architecture_refs")
   289→    for index, node in enumerate(nodes):
   290→        node_id = str(node.get("graph_node_id") or node.get("id") or f"nodes[{index}]")
   291→        for field in reference_fields:
   292→            for reference in node.get(field, []) if isinstance(node.get(field, []), list) else []:
   293→                if reference not in by_id:
   294→                    code = "dangling_dependency" if field == "depends_on" else "dangling_reference"
   295→                    findings.append({"node": node_id, "code": code, "detail": f"{field}:{reference}"})
   296→        parent = node.get("parent_feature")
   297→        if isinstance(parent, str) and parent not in by_id:
   298→            findings.append({"node": node_id, "code": "dangling_reference", "detail": f"parent_feature:{parent}"})
   299→
   300→    state: dict[str, int] = {}
   301→
   302→    def visit(node_id: str) -> None:
   303→        if state.get(node_id) == 1:
   304→            findings.append({"node": node_id, "code": "dependency_cycle", "detail": node_id})
   305→            return
   306→        if state.get(node_id) == 2:
   307→            return
   308→        state[node_id] = 1
   309→        dependencies = by_id[node_id].get("depends_on", [])
   310→        for dependency in dependencies if isinstance(dependencies, list) else []:
   311→            if dependency in by_id:
   312→                visit(dependency)
   313→        state[node_id] = 2
   314→
   315→    for node_id in by_id:
   316→        visit(node_id)
   317→
   318→    children: dict[str, list[dict[str, Any]]] = defaultdict(list)
   319→    for node in nodes:
   320→        if isinstance(node.get("parent_feature"), str):
   321→            children[node["parent_feature"]].append(node)
   322→    order = {phase: int(phase[1:]) for phase in PHASES}
   323→    for feature_id, feature in ((node_id, node) for node_id, node in by_id.items() if node.get("artifact_kind") == "feature"):
   324→        members = children.get(feature_id, [])
   325→        if members or feature.get("status") == "done":
   326→            packages = {member.get("feature_package_id") for member in members}
   327→            phases = [member.get("phase_ref") for member in members]
   328→            if packages == {None} or len(packages) != 1 or set(phases) != PHASES or len(members) != 13:
   329→                findings.append({"node": feature_id, "code": "feature_package_not_exact_13", "detail": f"count={len(members)} phases={sorted(str(value) for value in set(phases))}"})
   330→        for child in members:
   331→            current = order.get(child.get("phase_ref"), 0)
   332→            for dependency in child.get("depends_on", []):
   333→                target = by_id.get(dependency, {})
   334→                if target.get("parent_feature") == feature_id and order.get(target.get("phase_ref"), 0) >= current:
   335→                    findings.append({"node": str(child.get("graph_node_id") or child.get("id") or "?"), "code": "non_forward_phase_dependency", "detail": dependency})
   336→        if feature.get("status") == "done":
   337→            if any(member.get("status") != "done" for member in members):
   338→                findings.append({"node": feature_id, "code": "premature_feature_done", "detail": "all 13 children must be done"})
   339→            for phase in {"P07", "P10", "P11"}:
   340→                child = next((member for member in members if member.get("phase_ref") == phase), {})
   341→                if not (child.get("completion_evidence") or {}).get("evidence_refs"):
   342→                    findings.append({"node": feature_id, "code": "feature_evidence_missing", "detail": phase})
   343→    return findings
   344→
   345→
   346→def validate(
   347→    nodes: list[dict[str, Any]], schema: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   348→    *, repo_root: Path | None = None, template_contract: dict[str, Any] | None = None,
   349→) -> list[dict[str, str]]:
   350→    canonical_schema = schema or load_json(SCHEMA_PATH)
   351→    if not isinstance(canonical_schema, dict):
   352→        raise ContractError(f"canonical graph schema must be an object: {SCHEMA_PATH}")
   353→    contract = template_contract or load_json(TEMPLATE_CONTRACT_PATH)
   354→    if not isinstance(contract, dict):
   355→        raise ContractError(f"template contract must be an object: {TEMPLATE_CONTRACT_PATH}")
   356→    findings = [item for index, node in enumerate(nodes) for item in schema_findings(node, canonical_schema, index)]
   357→    findings.extend(domain_findings(nodes))
   358→    findings.extend(artifact_findings(nodes, repo_root, contract))
   359→    return sorted(findings, key=lambda item: (item["node"], item["code"], item["detail"]))
   360→
   361→
   362→def main() -> int:
   363→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
   364→    parser.add_argument("--graph", required=True)
   365→    parser.add_argument("--repo-root")
   366→    args = parser.parse_args()
   367→    graph = Path(args.graph).expanduser().resolve(strict=True)
   368→    nodes = nodes_of(load_json(graph))
   369→    violations = validate(nodes, repo_root=_repo_root_for(graph, args.repo_root))
   370→    missing = sorted({
   371→        item["detail"] for item in violations
   372→        if item["code"] in {"frontmatter_missing", "artifact_missing"}
   373→        or (item["code"] == "schema_violation" and "required property" in item["detail"])
   374→    })
   375→    dump({
   376→        "valid": not violations,
   377→        "implementation_readiness": "complete" if not violations else "incomplete",
   378→        "missing_sections": missing,
   379→        "schema": str(SCHEMA_PATH),
   380→        "violations": violations,
   381→    })
   382→    return 0 if not violations else 1
   383→
   384→
   385→if __name__ == "__main__":
   386→    try:
   387→        raise SystemExit(main())
   388→    except (ContractError, OSError) as exc:
   389→        print(str(exc), file=sys.stderr)
   390→        raise SystemExit(2)
   391→

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→# /// script
     3→# name: status-graph
     4→# purpose: Query canonical dev-graph nodes and dependencies without mutating graph/content/external trackers.
     5→# inputs: ["argv: --repo-root PATH [filters] [--eval-log FILE]"]
     6→# outputs: ["stdout: JSON status report", "file: optional eval-log JSON receipt"]
     7→# requires-python = ">=3.10"
     8→# dependencies: ["validate-graph-schema.py"]
     9→# contexts: [A, B, C, E]
    10→# network: false
    11→# write-scope: optional eval-log receipt only
    12→# ///
    13→from __future__ import annotations
    14→
    15→import argparse
    16→import hashlib
    17→import importlib.util
    18→import json
    19→import sys
    20→from collections import defaultdict
    21→from pathlib import Path
    22→from typing import Any
    23→
    24→from _common import ContractError, atomic_json, contained, dump, load_json, repository_eval_root, utc_now
    25→from node_transaction import ensure_no_pending_transaction, graph_operation_lock
    26→
    27→
    28→def _validator() -> Any:
    29→    path = Path(__file__).with_name("validate-graph-schema.py")
    30→    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("dev_graph_status_validator", path)
    31→    if not spec or not spec.loader:
    32→        raise ContractError(f"cannot load canonical validator: {path}")
    33→    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    34→    sys.modules[spec.name] = module
    35→    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    36→    return module
    37→
    38→
    39→def _graph(root: Path, explicit: str | None) -> Path:
    40→    if explicit:
    41→        candidate = Path(explicit)
    42→        candidate = candidate if candidate.is_absolute() else root / candidate
    43→    else:
    44→        config = load_json(root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json")
    45→        raw = (config.get("local_state") or {}).get("graph") if isinstance(config, dict) else None
    46→        if not isinstance(raw, str) or not raw:
    47→            raise ContractError(".dev-graph/config.json omits local_state.graph")
    48→        candidate = root / raw
    49→    return contained(candidate, root, must_exist=True)
    50→
    51→
    52→def _node_id(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    53→    value = node.get("graph_node_id")
    54→    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
    55→        raise ContractError("graph node has no graph_node_id")
    56→    return value
    57→
    58→
    59→def _authority_digest(root: Path, graph_path: Path, nodes: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
    60→    paths = [root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json", graph_path]
    61→    for node in nodes:
    62→        raw = node.get("file_path")
    63→        if isinstance(raw, str):
    64→            paths.append(contained(root / raw, root, must_exist=True))
    65→    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    66→    for path in sorted({item.resolve(strict=True) for item in paths}, key=str):
    67→        digest.update(path.relative_to(root).as_posix().encode("utf-8"))
    68→        digest.update(b"\0")
    69→        digest.update(path.read_bytes())
    70→        digest.update(b"\0")
    71→    return digest.hexdigest()
    72→
    73→
    74→def _matches(node: dict[str, Any], args: argparse.Namespace) -> bool:
    75→    exact = {
    76→        "graph_node_id": args.id,
    77→        "artifact_kind": args.kind,
    78→        "project_id": args.project,
    79→        "domain": args.domain,
    80→        "status": args.status,
    81→    }
    82→    if any(expected is not None and node.get(key) != expected for key, expected in exact.items()):
    83→        return False
    84→    node_tags = set(node.get("tags", [])) if isinstance(node.get("tags"), list) else set()
    85→    requested_tags = set(args.tag)
    86→    if requested_tags:
    87→        if args.tag_match == "all" and not requested_tags <= node_tags:
    88→            return False
    89→        if args.tag_match == "any" and not requested_tags & node_tags:
    90→            return False
    91→    if args.keyword:
    92→        haystack = " ".join(
    93→            [
    94→                str(node.get("graph_node_id", "")),
    95→                str(node.get("title", "")),
    96→                str(node.get("domain", "")),
    97→                " ".join(str(value) for value in node.get("tags", [])),
    98→            ]
    99→        ).casefold()
   100→        if args.keyword.casefold() not in haystack:
   101→            return False
   102→    return True
   103→
   104→
   105→def _report_locked(args: argparse.Namespace, root: Path, graph_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
   106→    ensure_no_pending_transaction(graph_path)
   107→    before = graph_path.read_bytes()
   108→    data = load_json(graph_path)
   109→    nodes = data.get("nodes") if isinstance(data, dict) else None
   110→    if not isinstance(nodes, list) or not all(isinstance(node, dict) for node in nodes):
   111→        raise ContractError("canonical graph must contain nodes[] objects")
   112→    authority_before = _authority_digest(root, graph_path, nodes)
   113→    validator = _validator()
   114→    violations = validator.validate(nodes, repo_root=root)
   115→    if violations:
   116→        raise ContractError(f"canonical graph validation failed: {json.dumps(violations, ensure_ascii=False)}")
   117→
   118→    dependents: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
   119→    for node in nodes:
   120→        node_id = _node_id(node)
   121→        for dependency in node.get("depends_on", []):
   122→            dependents[str(dependency)].append(node_id)
   123→    results = []
   124→    for node in sorted((item for item in nodes if _matches(item, args)), key=_node_id):
   125→        node_id = _node_id(node)
   126→        results.append(
   127→            {
   128→                "graph_node_id": node_id,
   129→                "artifact_kind": node.get("artifact_kind"),
   130→                "project_id": node.get("project_id"),
   131→                "domain": node.get("domain"),
   132→                "tags": node.get("tags", []),
   133→                "title": node.get("title"),
   134→                "file_path": node.get("file_path"),
   135→                "status": node.get("status"),
   136→                "closed_at": (node.get("completion_evidence") or {}).get("completed_at"),
   137→                "depends_on": node.get("depends_on", []),
   138→                "dependents": sorted(dependents.get(node_id, [])),
   139→                "parent_feature": node.get("parent_feature"),
   140→                "feature_package_id": node.get("feature_package_id"),
   141→                "tracker_binding": node.get("tracker_binding"),
   142→                "beads_linkage": node.get("beads_linkage"),
   143→                "issue_linkage": node.get("issue_linkage"),
   144→                "execution_contexts": node.get("execution_contexts", []),
   145→            }
   146→        )
   147→    after = graph_path.read_bytes()
   148→    before_digest = hashlib.sha256(before).hexdigest()
   149→    after_digest = hashlib.sha256(after).hexdigest()
   150→    if before_digest != after_digest:
   151→        raise ContractError("status query changed the canonical graph")
   152→    authority_after = _authority_digest(root, graph_path, nodes)
   153→    if authority_before != authority_after:
   154→        raise ContractError("status query changed graph/config/content authority")
   155→    report = {
   156→        "ok": True,
   157→        "read_only": True,
   158→        "graph": graph_path.relative_to(root).as_posix(),
   159→        "graph_sha256_before": before_digest,
   160→        "graph_sha256_after": after_digest,
   161→        "authority_sha256_before": authority_before,
   162→        "authority_sha256_after": authority_after,
   163→        "filters": {
   164→            "id": args.id,
   165→            "kind": args.kind,
   166→            "project": args.project,
   167→            "domain": args.domain,
   168→            "status": args.status,
   169→            "tags": args.tag,
   170→            "tag_match": args.tag_match,
   171→            "keyword": args.keyword,
   172→        },
   173→        "count": len(results),
   174→        "results": results,
   175→        "executed_at": utc_now(),
   176→    }
   177→    if args.eval_log:
   178→        eval_root = repository_eval_root(root)
   179→        target = Path(args.eval_log)
   180→        target = target if target.is_absolute() else root / target
   181→        target = contained(target, eval_root, must_exist=False)
   182→        atomic_json(target, report)
   183→        report["eval_log"] = target.relative_to(root).as_posix()
   184→    return report
   185→
   186→
   187→def _report(args: argparse.Namespace) -> dict[str, Any]:
   188→    root = Path(args.repo_root).expanduser().resolve(strict=True)
   189→    graph_path = _graph(root, args.graph)
   190→    with graph_operation_lock(graph_path, exclusive=False):
   191→        return _report_locked(args, root, graph_path)
   192→
   193→
   194→def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
   195→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Read-only dev-graph status query")
   196→    parser.add_argument("--repo-root", default=".")
   197→    parser.add_argument("--graph")
   198→    parser.add_argument("--id")
   199→    parser.add_argument("--kind", choices=("issue", "task", "specification", "architecture", "document", "feature"))
   200→    parser.add_argument("--project")
   201→    parser.add_argument("--domain")
   202→    parser.add_argument("--status")
   203→    parser.add_argument("--tag", action="append", default=[])
   204→    parser.add_argument("--tag-match", choices=("all", "any"), default="all")
   205→    parser.add_argument("--keyword")
   206→    parser.add_argument("--eval-log", default="eval-log/run-dev-graph-status-execution.json")
   207→    parser.add_argument("--no-eval-log", action="store_true")
   208→    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
   209→    if args.no_eval_log:
   210→        args.eval_log = None
   211→    try:
   212→        dump(_report(args))
   213→        return 0
   214→    except (ContractError, OSError, json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
   215→        dump({"ok": False, "read_only": True, "error": str(exc)})
   216→        return 2
   217→
   218→
   219→if __name__ == "__main__":
   220→    raise SystemExit(main())
   221→

#!/usr/bin/env python3
# /// script
# name: status-graph
# purpose: Query canonical dev-graph nodes and dependencies without mutating graph/content/external trackers.
# inputs: ["argv: --repo-root PATH [filters] [--eval-log FILE]"]
# outputs: ["stdout: JSON status report", "file: optional eval-log JSON receipt"]
# requires-python = ">=3.10"
# dependencies: ["validate-graph-schema.py"]
# contexts: [A, B, C, E]
# network: false
# write-scope: optional eval-log receipt only
# ///
from __future__ import annotations

import argparse
import hashlib
import importlib.util
import json
import sys
from collections import defaultdict
from pathlib import Path
from typing import Any

from _common import ContractError, atomic_json, contained, dump, load_json, repository_eval_root, utc_now
from node_transaction import ensure_no_pending_transaction, graph_operation_lock


def _validator() -> Any:
    path = Path(__file__).with_name("validate-graph-schema.py")
    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("dev_graph_status_validator", path)
    if not spec or not spec.loader:
        raise ContractError(f"cannot load canonical validator: {path}")
    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    sys.modules[spec.name] = module
    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    return module


def _graph(root: Path, explicit: str | None) -> Path:
    if explicit:
        candidate = Path(explicit)
        candidate = candidate if candidate.is_absolute() else root / candidate
    else:
        config = load_json(root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json")
        raw = (config.get("local_state") or {}).get("graph") if isinstance(config, dict) else None
        if not isinstance(raw, str) or not raw:
            raise ContractError(".dev-graph/config.json omits local_state.graph")
        candidate = root / raw
    return contained(candidate, root, must_exist=True)


def _node_id(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    value = node.get("graph_node_id")
    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
        raise ContractError("graph node has no graph_node_id")
    return value


def _authority_digest(root: Path, graph_path: Path, nodes: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
    paths = [root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json", graph_path]
    for node in nodes:
        raw = node.get("file_path")
        if isinstance(raw, str):
            paths.append(contained(root / raw, root, must_exist=True))
    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    for path in sorted({item.resolve(strict=True) for item in paths}, key=str):
        digest.update(path.relative_to(root).as_posix().encode("utf-8"))
        digest.update(b"\0")
        digest.update(path.read_bytes())
        digest.update(b"\0")
    return digest.hexdigest()


def _matches(node: dict[str, Any], args: argparse.Namespace) -> bool:
    exact = {
        "graph_node_id": args.id,
        "artifact_kind": args.kind,
        "project_id": args.project,
        "domain": args.domain,
        "status": args.status,
    }
    if any(expected is not None and node.get(key) != expected for key, expected in exact.items()):
        return False
    node_tags = set(node.get("tags", [])) if isinstance(node.get("tags"), list) else set()
    requested_tags = set(args.tag)
    if requested_tags:
        if args.tag_match == "all" and not requested_tags <= node_tags:
            return False
        if args.tag_match == "any" and not requested_tags & node_tags:
            return False
    if args.keyword:
        haystack = " ".join(
            [
                str(node.get("graph_node_id", "")),
                str(node.get("title", "")),
                str(node.get("domain", "")),
                " ".join(str(value) for value in node.get("tags", [])),
            ]
        ).casefold()
        if args.keyword.casefold() not in haystack:
            return False
    return True


def _report_locked(args: argparse.Namespace, root: Path, graph_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
    ensure_no_pending_transaction(graph_path)
    before = graph_path.read_bytes()
    data = load_json(graph_path)
    nodes = data.get("nodes") if isinstance(data, dict) else None
    if not isinstance(nodes, list) or not all(isinstance(node, dict) for node in nodes):
        raise ContractError("canonical graph must contain nodes[] objects")
    authority_before = _authority_digest(root, graph_path, nodes)
    validator = _validator()
    violations = validator.validate(nodes, repo_root=root)
    if violations:
        raise ContractError(f"canonical graph validation failed: {json.dumps(violations, ensure_ascii=False)}")

    dependents: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
    for node in nodes:
        node_id = _node_id(node)
        for dependency in node.get("depends_on", []):
            dependents[str(dependency)].append(node_id)
    results = []
    for node in sorted((item for item in nodes if _matches(item, args)), key=_node_id):
        node_id = _node_id(node)
        results.append(
            {
                "graph_node_id": node_id,
                "artifact_kind": node.get("artifact_kind"),
                "project_id": node.get("project_id"),
                "domain": node.get("domain"),
                "tags": node.get("tags", []),
                "title": node.get("title"),
                "file_path": node.get("file_path"),
                "status": node.get("status"),
                "closed_at": (node.get("completion_evidence") or {}).get("completed_at"),
                "depends_on": node.get("depends_on", []),
                "dependents": sorted(dependents.get(node_id, [])),
                "parent_feature": node.get("parent_feature"),
                "feature_package_id": node.get("feature_package_id"),
                "tracker_binding": node.get("tracker_binding"),
                "beads_linkage": node.get("beads_linkage"),
                "issue_linkage": node.get("issue_linkage"),
                "execution_contexts": node.get("execution_contexts", []),
            }
        )
    after = graph_path.read_bytes()
    before_digest = hashlib.sha256(before).hexdigest()
    after_digest = hashlib.sha256(after).hexdigest()
    if before_digest != after_digest:
        raise ContractError("status query changed the canonical graph")
    authority_after = _authority_digest(root, graph_path, nodes)
    if authority_before != authority_after:
        raise ContractError("status query changed graph/config/content authority")
    report = {
        "ok": True,
        "read_only": True,
        "graph": graph_path.relative_to(root).as_posix(),
        "graph_sha256_before": before_digest,
        "graph_sha256_after": after_digest,
        "authority_sha256_before": authority_before,
        "authority_sha256_after": authority_after,
        "filters": {
            "id": args.id,
            "kind": args.kind,
            "project": args.project,
            "domain": args.domain,
            "status": args.status,
            "tags": args.tag,
            "tag_match": args.tag_match,
            "keyword": args.keyword,
        },
        "count": len(results),
        "results": results,
        "executed_at": utc_now(),
    }
    if args.eval_log:
        eval_root = repository_eval_root(root)
        target = Path(args.eval_log)
        target = target if target.is_absolute() else root / target
        target = contained(target, eval_root, must_exist=False)
        atomic_json(target, report)
        report["eval_log"] = target.relative_to(root).as_posix()
    return report


def _report(args: argparse.Namespace) -> dict[str, Any]:
    root = Path(args.repo_root).expanduser().resolve(strict=True)
    graph_path = _graph(root, args.graph)
    with graph_operation_lock(graph_path, exclusive=False):
        return _report_locked(args, root, graph_path)


def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Read-only dev-graph status query")
    parser.add_argument("--repo-root", default=".")
    parser.add_argument("--graph")
    parser.add_argument("--id")
    parser.add_argument("--kind", choices=("issue", "task", "specification", "architecture", "document", "feature"))
    parser.add_argument("--project")
    parser.add_argument("--domain")
    parser.add_argument("--status")
    parser.add_argument("--tag", action="append", default=[])
    parser.add_argument("--tag-match", choices=("all", "any"), default="all")
    parser.add_argument("--keyword")
    parser.add_argument("--eval-log", default="eval-log/run-dev-graph-status-execution.json")
    parser.add_argument("--no-eval-log", action="store_true")
    args = parser.parse_args(argv)
    if args.no_eval_log:
        args.eval_log = None
    try:
        dump(_report(args))
        return 0
    except (ContractError, OSError, json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError, ValueError) as exc:
        dump({"ok": False, "read_only": True, "error": str(exc)})
        return 2


if __name__ == "__main__":
    raise SystemExit(main())

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture. I need to:

1. Set up the r6-status fixture directory with:
   - `.dev-graph/config.json`
   - `.dev-graph/state/graph.json` (initially empty with no nodes)
   - Content root directories
   - Staging JSON files for upsert-node.py
   
2. Use `upsert-node.py` to add nodes to the graph

3. Take pre-snapshots of graph/config

4. Run the skill `dev-graph:run-dev-graph-status` via the Skill tool
   
5. Then verify the four conditions: the graph hash stays consistent before and after, the status summary isn't empty, the schema validation passes, and the goal-seek artifacts are generated

I'll model this after the r5-status fixture but with new node IDs for r6. The fixture needs to live in a git repository, and I'll invoke the skill with `--repo-root` pointing to that directory.

But there's a complication—when `resolve-repo-context.py` runs, it discovers both the explicit `--repo-root` I pass and the actual git root from `git rev-parse --show-toplevel`. Since the fixture sits inside the worktree, these two paths won't match, and the check `root != actual` will fail. needs to either be a git repository itself or the git toplevel needs to match the fixture path. Let me check if I should initialize the fixture as its own git repo or if there's another way to handle this validation. I need to re-read the task requirements more carefully. The fixture needs to be prepared first with the dev-graph canonical graph and config following the C02 contract, using only upsert-node.py for graph.json modifications. Before I can invoke the skill, the fixture directory itself needs to be a proper git repository. Let me check the discover function in resolve-repo-context.py to understand how it determines the repo root when an explicit path is provided. I see the issue now—the fixture needs to be a proper git repository itself. So I'll initialize git in the fixture directory, then set up the .dev-graph configuration and graph.json file. After that, I can use the upsert-node script to add nodes, create the eval-log directory, and finally run the skill. Let me think through the exact directory structure and initialization steps needed.

content.thinking:
Good. Now I have a full understanding. Let me prepare the fixture step by step:

1. `git init` the fixture directory
2. Create `.dev-graph/config.json`
3. Create `.dev-graph/state/graph.json` with empty nodes
4. Create content root directories
5. Create staging JSON files for nodes
6. Use `upsert-node.py` to add nodes
7. Create `eval-log/` directory
8. `git add` and `git commit` everything so the fixture is a proper git repo

Then:
- Take pre-snapshot of graph/config hashes
- Run the skill via Skill tool
- Take post-snapshot and verify the verification criteria
- Write the result

I'll base the fixture on the r5-status fixture but adapt it with r6 IDs. One thing I need to clarify: when I invoke the skill, it should handle the goal-seek wiring automatically as part of its execution protocol, so I don't need to wire that manually.

My workflow is to prepare the fixture, capture the pre-snapshot of hashes, invoke the skill using the Skill tool, capture the post-snapshot, verify the criteria, and document the results. The key difference from r5 is that I need to ensure the full goal-seek wiring from SKILL.md is executed rather than bypassing it by calling the underlying script directly. Now I'll start building out the fixture directory structure and running through the setup process.

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/dm/dev/dev/個人開発/HarnessHub/.worktrees/task-20260720-203343-wt-3/plugins/dev-graph/skills/run-dev-graph-status

# run-dev-graph-status

## Purpose & Output Contract

- 入力: C24/C11 で read-only 検証済み graph store、scope/filter/tag match mode。
- 出力: node identity、classification、path、status/closed_at、依存関係、feature/tracker linkage を持つ検索 report。
- 完了条件: report が graph 実状態と一致し、実行前後の graph/config/content digest が不変で外部副作用0である。

C24 で caller root を固定し、C11 の read-only validation 後に graph store を検索する。全 filter は AND、複数 tag は明示された match mode に従う。結果は `graph_node_id/artifact_kind/project_id/domain/tags/file_path/status/closed_at/depends_on/dependents/parent_feature/feature_package_id/tracker_binding/linkage` を返す。

`status-graph.py`で検索前後のgraph/config/content authority digestを比較し、変化があれば失敗する。GitHub/Beads command、writer、render、syncを呼ばない。該当なしは空resultの成功、schema不正・root外file_path・dangling dependency・pending node WALは診断付き失敗。`--eval-log`はrealpathをrepositoryの`eval-log/`配下へ限定し、それ以外への書込みを拒否する。

```bash
python3 ../../scripts/status-graph.py \
  --repo-root "$DEV_GRAPH_ROOT" \
  --graph "$DEV_GRAPH_ROOT/.dev-graph/state/graph.json" \
  <filters>
```

## ゴールシーク実行

### ゴール (Goal)

特定のissue/task/specification/architecture/documentをgraph_node_id/artifact_kind/project_id/domain/status/tagsで検索し、依存関係・close/tombstone状態をread-onlyで確認できる状態になっている

### 目的・背景 (Why)

特定ノードの検索・状態確認 (要件C11) を独立の読取り専用経路として提供しないと、状態確認のたびに sync/render 相当の重い処理を経由せざるを得ず、副作用のない検索操作が書込みを伴う操作と混同されるリスクがあるため

### 完了チェックリスト

- [ ] query は許可 filter field と明示 tag match mode だけを持つ
- [ ] `validate-graph-schema.py` が exit0 で root 外 file_path と dangling dependency が0件である
- [ ] report の status/closed_at/depends_on/dependents/linkage が graph snapshot と一致する
- [ ] 該当なしが空 result の成功として返る
- [ ] 実行前後の graph/config/content digest が同一で外部 write が0件である
- [ ] 全読取り中の共有lock、pending WAL拒否、eval-log root/target realpath containmentが受入テストで確認される

### ゴールシークループ

frontmatter の `goal_seek.engine: inline` / `fork: subagent` / `max_loops: 5` を実行契約とする。固定手順は使わず、未達 checklist と担当 `prompts/*.md` からその周回の操作を都度生成する。各周回で inner criterion を検証し、完了後は outer criterion の live trial/content review を最大 `feedback_contract.max_iterations=3` 周で評価する。

### ゴールシーク配線

- 開始時に C24 `resolve-repo-context.py --mode read` の JSON receipt を得て、`repo_root` が `content_roots.repository` の realpath と一致する場合だけ `DEV_GRAPH_ROOT=<receipt.repo_root>` に固定する。cwd から再解決しない。
- 元のゴールを `$DEV_GRAPH_ROOT/eval-log/run-dev-graph-status-goal-spec.json` へ、各 checklist の status/evidence を `$DEV_GRAPH_ROOT/eval-log/run-dev-graph-status-progress.json` へ記録する。
- 未達 responsibility を担当する `prompts/<R-id>.md` を読み、`Agent` で分離 context に fork する。ユーザー判断が必要な境界だけ `AskUserQuestion` を使う。
- 各周回末に `$DEV_GRAPH_ROOT/eval-log/run-dev-graph-status-intermediate.jsonl` へ `original_goal`、`original_goal_hash`、`current_goal_snapshot`、`delta_from_original`、`merged_directive_for_next`、`drift_signal` を append-only で記録する。次周回は直前の `merged_directive_for_next` を必須入力にする。
- 5周到達時に未達が残れば完了扱いせず、progress と blocker を親へ handoff する。全 checklist と `feedback_contract.criteria` が PASS のときだけ完了する。

### ゴールシーク検証

各周回後に次の検査を実行し、中間成果物の欠落・goal drift・hash 不一致を fail-closed にする。

```bash
python3 - "$DEV_GRAPH_ROOT/eval-log/run-dev-graph-status-goal-spec.json" "$DEV_GRAPH_ROOT/eval-log/run-dev-graph-status-intermediate.jsonl" <<'PY'
import hashlib, json, sys
goal = json.load(open(sys.argv[1], encoding='utf-8'))
rows = [json.loads(line) for line in open(sys.argv[2], encoding='utf-8') if line.strip()]
required_keys = {'original_goal','original_goal_hash','current_goal_snapshot','delta_from_original','merged_directive_for_next','drift_signal'}
expected = hashlib.sha256(goal['original_goal'].encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()
assert rows, 'intermediate.jsonl is empty'
for row in rows:
    assert required_keys <= row.keys(), required_keys - row.keys()
    assert row['original_goal'] == goal['original_goal']
    assert row['original_goal_hash'] == expected
PY
```

## Criteria acceptance

- `criteria:IN1`: `validate-graph-schema.py`後の結果は必須キー欠落が0件である。
- `criteria:OUT1`: reportの`status/closed_at/依存関係`はgraphの実状態と一致する。
- `criteria:OUT2`: 全処理中の共有lockとpending WAL検査、実行前後のdigestを比較し、eval-log root/targetのrealpath containmentを確認して、`eval-log/`以外のgraph/content/GitHub副作用0件を保証する。

## Gotchas

- filter 間は OR に緩めず、全て AND で適用する。複数 tag は指定された match mode に従う。
- node 本文から status を推測せず、graph の `status/closed_at` をそのまま報告する。
- 該当なしは空 result の成功とし、schema 不正や dangling dependency と混同しない。
- writer、sync、render、GitHub/Beads command を呼び出さない。

# Prompt: R1-elicit

> 検索条件 (id/種別/キーワード) と表示したい状態範囲をヒアリングして確定する

## Layer 1: 基本定義層

- `responsibility_id`: `R1-elicit`
- `skill`: `run-dev-graph-status`
- 不変目的: 検索条件 (id/種別/キーワード) と表示したい状態範囲をヒアリングして確定する
- 成功条件は Layer 2 の受入条件と Layer 5 の二値 checklist の同時充足とする。

## Layer 2: ドメイン層

### 入力契約

- id/kind/project/domain/status/tags/keyword filter、tag mode、repo context。

### 出力契約

- 許可fieldだけのnormalized read-only query。

### 責務境界

- writer/sync/render/external操作を要求せず任意pathを入力にしない。

### 受入条件

- filterがschema語彙適合、未知field 0、tag mode明示になる。

## Layer 3: インフラ層

- 使用資産: ReadとAskUserQuestion。
- path は caller repository context または skill-relative reference から解決し、環境固有の絶対 path を成果物へ保存しない。

## Layer 4: 共通ポリシー層

- 入力契約、authority、containment、schema のいずれかが未達なら fail-closed とし、部分成功を PASS にしない。
- secret と認証情報を prompt 出力、graph、receipt に埋め込まない。
- 同一入力と同一 revision/digest では同じ decision と output shape を返す。

## Layer 5: エージェント層 (l5-contract v2.0.0)

### 5.1 担当 agent

- `run-dev-graph-status/R1-elicit`。重い判断または独立検証は `Agent` で分離 context に fork する。

### 5.2 ゴール定義

- 目的: 検索条件 (id/種別/キーワード) と表示したい状態範囲をヒアリングして確定する
- 背景: この責務を隣接 responsibility から分離し、入力・出力・authority を一意にする。
- 達成ゴール: 許可fieldだけのnormalized read-only queryが生成され、受入条件を満たした状態になっている。

### 5.3 完了チェックリスト (ゴール到達の停止条件)

- [ ] 宣言した入力が全て検証済みである
- [ ] 出力が宣言した shape と authority を満たす
- [ ] 責務境界に反する read/write/delegation が0件である
- [ ] filterがschema語彙適合、未知field 0、tag mode明示になる

### 5.4 実行方式

- 固定手順を持たない。未達 checklist を評価し、操作を都度立案・実行・検証する。各周回末に `original_goal`、`delta_from_original`、`merged_directive_for_next`、`drift_signal` を追記し、最大5周で未達なら上位 skill へ fail-closed で返す。

## Layer 6: オーケストレーション層

- R2へqueryを渡す。
- 前段 receipt/digest と後段 input digest を一致させ、stale handoff を拒否する。

## Layer 7: UserInput

- 不足情報が実行結果を変える場合だけ `AskUserQuestion` を使う。repo policy で決まる値、保存先、secret、node ID は質問しない。
- ユーザー提示は日本語、schema key/CLI parameter は原語を保つ。

## 出力指示

Layer 2 の入力・出力・責務境界・受入条件を正本としてこの単一責務だけを実行し、思考過程を出力せず、artifact/receipt、検証結果、未達 blocker だけを返す。

file.content:
# Prompt: R3-status

> validate-graph-schema.py で読み取ったグラフストアから該当ノードを検索し、status/closed_at/依存関係を含む状態表示レポートを返す (eval-log/以外へは書き込まない)

## Layer 1: 基本定義層

- `responsibility_id`: `R3-status`
- `skill`: `run-dev-graph-status`
- 不変目的: validate-graph-schema.py で読み取ったグラフストアから該当ノードを検索し、status/closed_at/依存関係を含む状態表示レポートを返す (書込みは行わない)
- 成功条件は Layer 2 の受入条件と Layer 5 の二値 checklist の同時充足とする。

## Layer 2: ドメイン層

### 入力契約

- graph digest、query plan、検索前content/config digest。

### 出力契約

- node status/dependencies/linkage reportと検索後digest比較。

### 責務境界

- Write/Edit/external mutationを呼ばずroot外file_pathを表示しない。

### 受入条件

- reportが実状態一致、前後digest同一、external write 0になる。

## Layer 3: インフラ層

- 使用資産: Readとvalidate-graph-schema。
- path は caller repository context または skill-relative reference から解決し、環境固有の絶対 path を成果物へ保存しない。

## Layer 4: 共通ポリシー層

- 入力契約、authority、containment、schema のいずれかが未達なら fail-closed とし、部分成功を PASS にしない。
- secret と認証情報を prompt 出力、graph、receipt に埋め込まない。
- 同一入力と同一 revision/digest では同じ decision と output shape を返す。

## Layer 5: エージェント層 (l5-contract v2.0.0)

### 5.1 担当 agent

- `run-dev-graph-status/R3-status`。重い判断または独立検証は `Agent` で分離 context に fork する。

### 5.2 ゴール定義

- 目的: validate-graph-schema.py で読み取ったグラフストアから該当ノードを検索し、status/closed_at/依存関係を含む状態表示レポートを返す (書込みは行わない)
- 背景: この責務を隣接 responsibility から分離し、入力・出力・authority を一意にする。
- 達成ゴール: node status/dependencies/linkage reportと検索後digest比較が生成され、受入条件を満たした状態になっている。

### 5.3 完了チェックリスト (ゴール到達の停止条件)

- [ ] 宣言した入力が全て検証済みである
- [ ] 出力が宣言した shape と authority を満たす
- [ ] 責務境界に反する read/write/delegation が0件である
- [ ] reportが実状態一致、前後digest同一、external write 0になる

### 5.4 実行方式

- 固定手順を持たない。未達 checklist を評価し、操作を都度立案・実行・検証する。各周回末に `original_goal`、`delta_from_original`、`merged_directive_for_next`、`drift_signal` を追記し、最大5周で未達なら上位 skill へ fail-closed で返す。

## Layer 6: オーケストレーション層

- read-only reportを返す。
- 前段 receipt/digest と後段 input digest を一致させ、stale handoff を拒否する。

## Layer 7: UserInput

- 不足情報が実行結果を変える場合だけ `AskUserQuestion` を使う。repo policy で決まる値、保存先、secret、node ID は質問しない。
- ユーザー提示は日本語、schema key/CLI parameter は原語を保つ。

## 出力指示

Layer 2 の入力・出力・責務境界・受入条件を正本としてこの単一責務だけを実行し、思考過程を出力せず、artifact/receipt、検証結果、未達 blocker だけを返す。

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→# /// script
     3→# name: status-graph
     4→# purpose: Query canonical dev-graph nodes and dependencies without mutating graph/content/external trackers.
     5→# inputs: ["argv: --repo-root PATH [filters] [--eval-log FILE]"]
     6→# outputs: ["stdout: JSON status report", "file: optional eval-log JSON receipt"]
     7→# requires-python = ">=3.10"
     8→# dependencies: ["validate-graph-schema.py"]
     9→# contexts: [A, B, C, E]
    10→# network: false
    11→# write-scope: optional eval-log receipt only
    12→# ///
    13→from __future__ import annotations
    14→
    15→import argparse
    16→import hashlib
    17→import importlib.util
    18→import json
    19→import sys
    20→from collections import defaultdict
    21→from pathlib import Path
    22→from typing import Any
    23→
    24→from _common import ContractError, atomic_json, contained, dump, load_json, repository_eval_root, utc_now
    25→from node_transaction import ensure_no_pending_transaction, graph_operation_lock
    26→
    27→
    28→def _validator() -> Any:
    29→    path = Path(__file__).with_name("validate-graph-schema.py")
    30→    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("dev_graph_status_validator", path)
    31→    if not spec or not spec.loader:
    32→        raise ContractError(f"cannot load canonical validator: {path}")
    33→    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    34→    sys.modules[spec.name] = module
    35→    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    36→    return module
    37→
    38→
    39→def _graph(root: Path, explicit: str | None) -> Path:
    40→    if explicit:
    41→        candidate = Path(explicit)
    42→        candidate = candidate if candidate.is_absolute() else root / candidate
    43→    else:
    44→        config = load_json(root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json")
    45→        raw = (config.get("local_state") or {}).get("graph") if isinstance(config, dict) else None
    46→        if not isinstance(raw, str) or not raw:
    47→            raise ContractError(".dev-graph/config.json omits local_state.graph")
    48→        candidate = root / raw
    49→    return contained(candidate, root, must_exist=True)
    50→
    51→
    52→def _node_id(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    53→    value = node.get("graph_node_id")
    54→    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
    55→        raise ContractError("graph node has no graph_node_id")
    56→    return value
    57→
    58→
    59→def _authority_digest(root: Path, graph_path: Path, nodes: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
    60→    paths = [root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json", graph_path]
    61→    for node in nodes:
    62→        raw = node.get("file_path")
    63→        if isinstance(raw, str):
    64→            paths.append(contained(root / raw, root, must_exist=True))
    65→    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    66→    for path in sorted({item.resolve(strict=True) for item in paths}, key=str):
    67→        digest.update(path.relative_to(root).as_posix().encode("utf-8"))
    68→        digest.update(b"\0")
    69→        digest.update(path.read_bytes())
    70→        digest.update(b"\0")
    71→    return digest.hexdigest()
    72→
    73→
    74→def _matches(node: dict[str, Any], args: argparse.Namespace) -> bool:
    75→    exact = {
    76→        "graph_node_id": args.id,
    77→        "artifact_kind": args.kind,
    78→        "project_id": args.project,
    79→        "domain": args.domain,
    80→        "status": args.status,

1→#!/usr/bin/env python3
     2→# /// script
     3→# name: status-graph
     4→# purpose: Query canonical dev-graph nodes and dependencies without mutating graph/content/external trackers.
     5→# inputs: ["argv: --repo-root PATH [filters] [--eval-log FILE]"]
     6→# outputs: ["stdout: JSON status report", "file: optional eval-log JSON receipt"]
     7→# requires-python = ">=3.10"
     8→# dependencies: ["validate-graph-schema.py"]
     9→# contexts: [A, B, C, E]
    10→# network: false
    11→# write-scope: optional eval-log receipt only
    12→# ///
    13→from __future__ import annotations
    14→
    15→import argparse
    16→import hashlib
    17→import importlib.util
    18→import json
    19→import sys
    20→from collections import defaultdict
    21→from pathlib import Path
    22→from typing import Any
    23→
    24→from _common import ContractError, atomic_json, contained, dump, load_json, repository_eval_root, utc_now
    25→from node_transaction import ensure_no_pending_transaction, graph_operation_lock
    26→
    27→
    28→def _validator() -> Any:
    29→    path = Path(__file__).with_name("validate-graph-schema.py")
    30→    spec = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("dev_graph_status_validator", path)
    31→    if not spec or not spec.loader:
    32→        raise ContractError(f"cannot load canonical validator: {path}")
    33→    module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(spec)
    34→    sys.modules[spec.name] = module
    35→    spec.loader.exec_module(module)
    36→    return module
    37→
    38→
    39→def _graph(root: Path, explicit: str | None) -> Path:
    40→    if explicit:
    41→        candidate = Path(explicit)
    42→        candidate = candidate if candidate.is_absolute() else root / candidate
    43→    else:
    44→        config = load_json(root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json")
    45→        raw = (config.get("local_state") or {}).get("graph") if isinstance(config, dict) else None
    46→        if not isinstance(raw, str) or not raw:
    47→            raise ContractError(".dev-graph/config.json omits local_state.graph")
    48→        candidate = root / raw
    49→    return contained(candidate, root, must_exist=True)
    50→
    51→
    52→def _node_id(node: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    53→    value = node.get("graph_node_id")
    54→    if not isinstance(value, str) or not value:
    55→        raise ContractError("graph node has no graph_node_id")
    56→    return value
    57→
    58→
    59→def _authority_digest(root: Path, graph_path: Path, nodes: list[dict[str, Any]]) -> str:
    60→    paths = [root / ".dev-graph" / "config.json", graph_path]
    61→    for node in nodes:
    62→        raw = node.get("file_path")
    63→        if isinstance(raw, str):
    64→            paths.append(contained(root / raw, root, must_exist=True))
    65→    digest = hashlib.sha256()
    66→    for path in sorted({item.resolve(strict=True) for item in paths}, key=str):
    67→        digest.update(path.relative_to(root).as_posix().encode("utf-8"))
    68→        digest.update(b"\0")
    69→        digest.update(path.read_bytes())
    70→        digest.update(b"\0")
    71→    return digest.hexdigest()
    72→
    73→
    74→def _matches(node: dict[str, Any], args: argparse.Namespace) -> bool:
    75→    exact = {
    76→        "graph_node_id": args.id,
    77→        "artifact_kind": args.kind,
    78→        "project_id": args.project,
    79→        "domain": args.domain,
    80→        "status": args.status,
    81→    }
    82→    if any(expected is not None and node.get(key) != expected for key, expected in exact.items()):
    83→        return False
    84→    node_tags = set(node.get("tags", [])) if isinstance(node.get("tags"), list) else set()
    85→    requested_tags = set(args.tag)
    86→    if requested_tags:
    87→        if args.tag_match == "all" and not requested_tags <= node_tags:
    88→            return False
    89→        if args.tag_match == "any" and not requested_tags & node_tags:
    90→            return False
    91→    if args.keyword:
    92→        haystack = " ".join(
    93→            [
    94→                str(node.get("graph_node_id", "")),
    95→                str(node.get("title", "")),
    96→                str(node.get("domain", "")),
    97→                " ".join(str(value) for value in node.get("tags", [])),
    98→            ]
    99→        ).casefold()
   100→        if args.keyword.casefold() not in haystack:
   101→            return False
   102→    return True
   103→
   104→
   105→def _report_locked(args: argparse.Namespace, root: Path, graph_path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
   106→    ensure_no_pending_transaction(graph_path)
   107→    before = graph_path.read_bytes()
   108→    data = load_json(graph_path)
   109→    nodes = data.get("nodes") if isinstance(data, dict) else None
   110→    if not isinstance(nodes, list) or not all(isinstance(node, dict) for node in nodes):
   111→        raise ContractError("canonical graph must contain nodes[] objects")
   112→    authority_before = _authority_digest(root, graph_path, nodes)
   113→    validator = _validator()
   114→    violations = validator.validate(nodes, repo_root=root)
   115→    if violations:
   116→        raise ContractError(f"canonical graph validation failed: {json.dumps(violations, ensure_ascii=False)}")
   117→
   118→    dependents: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
   119→    for node in nodes:
   120→        node_id = _node_id(node)
   121→        for dependency in node.get("depends_on", []):
   122→            dependents[str(dependency)].append(node_id)
   123→    results = []
   124→    for node in sorted((item for item in nodes if _matches(item, args)), key=_node_id):
   125→        node_id = _node_id(node)
   126→        results.append(
   127→            {
   128→                "graph_node_id": node_id,
   129→                "artifact_kind": node.get("artifact_kind"),
   130→                "project_id": node.get("project_id"),
   131→                "domain": node.get("domain"),
   132→                "tags": node.get("tags", []),
   133→                "title": node.get("title"),
   134→                "file_path": node.get("file_path"),
   135→                "status": node.get("status"),
   136→                "closed_at": (node.get("completion_evidence") or {}).get("completed_at"),
   137→                "depends_on": node.get("depends_on", []),
   138→                "dependents": sorted(dependents.get(node_id, [])),
   139→                "parent_feature": node.get("parent_feature"),
   140→                "feature_package_id": node.get("feature_package_id"),
   141→                "tracker_binding": node.get("tracker_binding"),
   142→                "beads_linkage": node.get("beads_linkage"),
   143→                "issue_linkage": node.get("issue_linkage"),
   144→                "execution_contexts": node.get("execution_contexts", []),
   145→            }
   146→        )
   147→    after = graph_path.read_bytes()
   148→    before_digest = hashlib.sha256(before).hexdigest()
   149→    after_digest = hashlib.sha256(after).hexdigest()
   150→    if before_digest != after_digest:
   151→        raise ContractError("status query changed the canonical graph")
   152→    authority_after = _authority_digest(root, graph_path, nodes)
   153→    if authority_before != authority_after:
   154→        raise ContractError("status query changed graph/config/content authority")
   155→    report = {
   156→        "ok": True,
   157→        "read_only": True,
   158→        "graph": graph_path.relative_to(root).as_posix(),
   159→        "graph_sha256_before": before_digest,
   160→        "graph_sha256_after": after_digest,
   161→        "authority_sha256_before": authority_before,
   162→        "authority_sha256_after": authority_after,
   163→        "filters": {
   164→            "id": args.id,
   165→            "kind": args.kind,
   166→            "project": args.project,
   167→            "domain": args.domain,
   168→            "status": args.status,
   169→            "tags": args.tag,
   170→            "tag_match": args.tag_match,
   171→            "keyword": args.keyword,
   172→        },
   173→        "count": len(results),
   174→        "results": results,
   175→        "executed_at": utc_now(),
   176→    }
   177→    if args.eval_log:
   178→        eval_root = repository_eval_root(root)
   179→        target = Path(args.eval_log)
   180→        target = target if target.is_absolute() else root / target
   181→        target = contained(target, eval_root, must_exist=False)
   182→        atomic_json(target, report)
   183→        report["eval_log"] = target.relative_to(root).as_posix()
   184→    return report
   185→
   186→
   187→def _report(args: argparse.Namespace) -> dict[str, Any]:
   188→    root = Path(args.repo_root).expanduser().resolve(strict=True)
   189→    graph_path = _graph(root, args.graph)
   190→    with graph_operation_lock(graph_path, exclusive=False):
   191→        return _report_locked(args, root, graph_path)
   192→
   193→
   194→def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
   195→    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Read-only dev-graph status query")
   196→    parser.add_argument("--repo-root", default=".")
   197→    parser.add_argument("--graph")
   198→    parser.add_argument("--id")
   199→    parser.add_argument("--kind", choices=("issue", "task", "specification", "architecture", "document", "feature"))
   200→    parser.add_argument("--project")

    assert row['original_goal'] == goal['original_goal']
    assert row['original_goal_hash'] == expected
